56.reason: [Error: This condition uses ">" but the problem requires ">=" for right child, so equal values should go to the right child. As a result, the second 100 in the input is not inserted correctly, causing the leaf node 100 to be missing from the output.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing handling for num == p->data: according to the problem, duplicate values should go to the right child (right >= root), so this condition should be 'else' to include equality.]

91.reason: [Dereferencing `top` here is invalid because if the input has zero nodes, `top` points to an unused allocated block; but more critically, for non-zero inputs, `top` gets overwritten in `insert()`, so this assignment has no effect. Also, if `n == 0`, this causes undefined behavior as nothing is printed but tree traversal is attempted.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Dereferencing `top` here is invalid because if the input has zero nodes, `top` points to an unused allocated block; but more critically, for non-zero inputs, `top` gets overwritten in `insert()`, so this assignment has no effect. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Undefined behavior: 'top' points to allocated but uninitialized memory that may be overwritten; also, if first insert sets 'top = p', this value is lost]

100.reason: [If `n == 0`, `top` still points to the initially allocated dummy node (which was never properly initialized as a real tree node with `data`), leading to incorrect traversal. But more importantly, the insertion logic fails to handle duplicate values (`num == p->data`). According to the problem, right child should be used for values >= root, but the code only handles `<` and `>`, skipping insertion entirely when `num == p->data`. This causes the second `100` in the test case to be ignored, which is why the leaf node `100` (the duplicate) is missing from the output.]
